job_statement: When I need to completely remove GitOps from my cluster, I want to uninstall the Operator and all Argo CD instances, so I can ensure clean removal without orphaned resources.
prerequisites: Have cluster administrator privileges; Understand implications of removing GitOps workloads
related_jobs: Back up GitOps configurations before removal; Document applications managed by GitOps
desired_outcomes: Minimize orphaned resources after uninstallation; Ensure complete removal of all Argo CD instances; Reduce likelihood of namespace conflicts in future installations
evidence: removing_gitops-combined.adoc -> Uninstalling OpenShift GitOps, lines 1-9: Introduction explaining two-step process
notes: This is a consumption chain job (decommissioning). The documentation emphasizes that uninstalling the Operator alone will NOT remove Argo CD instances, highlighting the importance of the two-step process. Module provenance: ASSEMBLY (uninstalling-openshift-gitops.adoc).

job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when preparing to uninstall GitOps, I want to delete all Argo CD instances using the CLI, so I can ensure no orphaned instances remain before removing the Operator.
parent_job: Completely remove GitOps from my cluster
prerequisites: Have oc CLI access; Know all namespaces with Argo CD instances; Have backed up any important Argo CD configurations
related_jobs: Verify Argo CD instances are deleted; Check for remaining GitOps resources
desired_outcomes: Minimize time to identify all Argo CD instances; Ensure no instances are missed in default namespace; Reduce risk of partial uninstallation
evidence: removing_gitops-combined.adoc -> Deleting the Argo CD instances, lines 11-33: Procedure for deleting gitopsservice using oc CLI
notes: UI deletion is explicitly NOT supported for Argo CD instances. This must be done via CLI using 'oc delete gitopsservice cluster -n <namespace>'. The procedure emphasizes deleting from both openshift-gitops namespace and any other namespaces. Module provenance: PROCEDURE (go-deleting-argocd-instance.adoc).

job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when all Argo CD instances are removed, I want to uninstall the GitOps Operator via the web console, so I can complete the removal process.
parent_job: Completely remove GitOps from my cluster
prerequisites: Delete all Argo CD instances from all namespaces; Have web console access with admin privileges
related_jobs: Verify Operator is fully removed; Check for remaining CRDs or resources
desired_outcomes: Minimize steps required to uninstall Operator; Ensure clean Operator removal; Reduce likelihood of reinstallation conflicts
evidence: removing_gitops-combined.adoc -> Uninstalling the GitOps Operator, lines 35-48: Procedure for uninstalling via OperatorHub
notes: This is step 2 of the two-step process. The procedure uses the web console UI (Operators -> OperatorHub) to search for and uninstall the Red Hat OpenShift GitOps Operator. Additional resources section references general Operator deletion documentation. Module provenance: PROCEDURE (go-uninstalling-gitops-operator.adoc).
